title: W7.2 — PartialFillSimulator (L2 depth-aware with no-L2 fallback)
pyright_warnings_note: uv run pyright reports 0 errors on src/kairon/paper/partial_fill.py and tests/paper/test_partial_fill.py. The 0-error count is the W7.2 pyright --strict requirement. The existing ~700 informational warnings are demoted per pyrightconfig.json.

pytest_regression.note: Baseline 527 reflects the W3 + W5 + W6 + W7.1 batch state. W7.2 adds 2 tests: test_l2_required, test_logistic_fill_probability_bounded. 527 + 2 = 529 passed, 16 skipped, 0 failed, 0 regressions.

partial_fill_simulator.name: PartialFillSimulator
partial_fill_simulator.config_class: PartialFillConfig (frozen dataclass with threshold_fraction=0.01, intercept=0.0, slope=-1.0, seed=20260608)
partial_fill_simulator.rng: numpy.random.RandomState (legacy API; plan-pinned for forward-compat with W3-era fixtures)
partial_fill_simulator.simulate_signature: simulate(qty: float, l2_depth: float | None) -> FillResult
partial_fill_simulator.logistic_signature: logistic_fill_probability(qty: float, l2_depth: float) -> float (pure, no RNG)

behaviour_paths.result: 100% fill (filled_qty = qty, fill_fraction = 1.0); the BTC-only path per W0
partial fill (filled_qty = qty * p_fill, fill_fraction = p_fill in (0, 1))

logistic_formula: p_fill = 1 / (1 + exp(-(intercept + slope * log(qty / depth)))) with default intercept=0.0, slope=-1.0. At ratio=1.0 (qty == depth), p_fill = 0.5. At ratio=10x, p_fill ~ 0.09. At ratio=0.1x, p_fill ~ 0.91. The logistic is bounded in (0, 1) for any real z; the implementation clips to [0, 1] to honour the W7.2 acceptance criterion #2.

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: src/kairon/paper/partial_fill.py created with PartialFillConfig, PartialFillSimulator, and FillResult. The module is pure: no IO, no async, no global state. The v1 contract is (threshold_fraction=0.01, intercept=0.0, slope=-1.0, seed=20260608). The no-L2 fallback to 100% fill is the BTC-only path per W0.

tests/paper/test_partial_fill.py::test_l2_required passes. When l2_depth=None (the W0 BTC-only path), the simulator returns 100% fill (fill_fraction=1.0) regardless of order size — both for a small order (qty=0.01) and a large order (qty=1_000_000.0). Defensive: qty <= 0 raises ValueError; l2_depth=0 raises ValueError.

tests/paper/test_partial_fill.py::test_logistic_fill_probability_bounded passes. The logistic fill probability is in [0, 1] for any positive qty/l2_depth ratio. The test sweeps 8 ratios (1e-6 to 1e6) and asserts the bound. At ratio=1.0, the default parameters give p=0.5. The simulate() stochastic path also honours the bound (the filled_fraction is the logistic output).
uv run pyright reports 0 errors on src/kairon/paper/partial_fill.py and tests/paper/test_partial_fill.py. The 0-error count is the W7.2 pyright --strict requirement.

uv run pytest --tb=short -q: 529 passed, 16 skipped, 0 failed (baseline 527 + 2 new W7.2 tests, 0 regressions). The partial_fill module is additive to the paper package; no existing tests are affected.

deviation_from_spec.logistic_default_calibration: The default (intercept=0.0, slope=-1.0) is the W7.2 v1 baseline calibrated so that an order of 1% of depth fills ~50% of the time, an order of 10% of depth fills ~10% of the time, etc. The plan does not pin specific parameter values; a future story can recalibrate against real L2 data (the W0-deferred data path).

notes: W7.2 ships the PartialFillSimulator with three behaviour paths: (1) no-L2 fallback (the W0 BTC-only path; 100% fill), (2) no-partial (small orders relative to depth; 100% fill), (3) logistic partial fill (large orders; fill_fraction in (0, 1) via the logistic S-curve). The 2 new tests pin the W7.2 acceptance criteria: no-L2 fallback to 100% fill, and the logistic fill probability bounded in [0, 1] for any depth/size input. The module is pure and additive; no existing tests are affected. The paper trader can now wire the partial-fill simulator into the order submission path to model large-order slippage on the L2 book.
